name: DIY Corsi-Rosenthal Box
recommendations: A DIY Air Purifier That Works
positives: It is an air purifier you can build yourself with parts you might already have available at home
cost-effective DIY solution
You’ll be choosing the materials, so it’s super customizable and a fun project on its own
very high clean air delivery rate
Excellent filtration results
Excellent air cleaning performance for the price — one of the best ratios of performance to money spent
All parts are readily available on Amazon
Great for garages
Can be a fun project
sufficient for large open-concept spaces
negatives: no odor-absorbing carbon filter
It can run loudly and consume a lot of electricity, depending on the box fan you use
You don’t get the option to add a carbon filter to tackle VOCs and odors
It is a huge device and not the best looking — it takes up a lot of space
There are no extra features you’ll find on commercial devices, like a child lock or scheduling routines
Bulky
lacks particle sensors and Wi-Fi features
Loud
ugly and bulky design
very noisy operation
Must be put together

name: Levoit Vital 200S
positives: You can remove the pre-filter for easy cleaning
It comes with auto mode and app support with the VeSync app
wide air inlet prevents hair clogging
The filter has pelleted activated carbon, which is better than carbon-impregnated fabric
It doesn’t use an ionizer, so it is ozone-free
It offers great performance for an air purifier under $200
It is relatively quiet — it hits 57.7 dBA at its top speed, and its performance at speed 2 (41.9 dBA) is very good
good alternative if no ionizer is needed
The VeSync app unlocks smart modes and routines
negatives: It comes with an optical sensor rather than a laser sensor, so you can’t forget to clean it regularly
There is a big jump in how loud this thing gets when you go from speed 1 (37.6 dBA) to speed 2 (49.4 dBA)
slightly lower clean air delivery rate
It only has one fan speed below 45 decibels (speed 1) and its performance at this speed is weak (170 m3/h)

name: Levoit Core 200S
positives: Wi-Fi enabled with companion app for remote operation and schedules
sufficient CADR for bedroom/nursery
compact size fits on dresser or shelf
negatives: no automatic fan speed adjustment
poorer performance on smallest particles
lower air-cleaning rate than larger machines

name: Levoit Core Mini-P
recommendations: Best Value Air Purifier for Small Spaces
positives: Energy efficient
Operating costs are on average low
Very quiet
Portable size
negatives: No advanced features
So-so performance

name: Coway Airmega 100
positives: It comes with an onboard particle sensor that is highly responsive and unlocks auto-mode
It has low running costs thanks to affordable filter replacements and low electricity consumption
It offers better air cleaning performance at top speed than other small models
negatives: Speed 2 is just over our 45 dBA quiet performance limit, reaching 46.5 dBA
It produces a sort of whistling sound when running at its lowest fan speeds
The carbon-impregnated layer on the filter won’t make a significant difference against odors and gases

name: Levoit Core 300-P
positives: Decently affordable
Awesome price-to-performance ratio
Solid air cleaning
negatives: A smidge noisy
Not the most energy efficient

name: Coway Airmega Mighty AP-1512HH
positives: Three filters
Very effective for its price and size
Back handle makes it easy to move around
Ionizer to neutralize airborne particles
negatives: Air quality indicator light is confusing
Glossy finish attracts pet hair
Needs to be 8 inches from a wall or furniture
No app or remote control

name: Blueair Blue Pure 311i Max
positives: Energy efficient
Convenient mobile app
Impressive air cleaning performance
Low annual operating cost
negatives: A bit pricey
Bulky, no handles

name: Coway Airmega 350
positives: It offers excellent performance at top speed, outmatching large units like the Winix 9800 and the Xiaomi Elite
It looks like a million bucks
You can use it with any smart plug, so you can connect it to your smart home system or set simple automations
The filter replacements are fairly priced and can last up to 12 months
negatives: It comes with an optical sensor rather than a laser sensor, so you can’t forget to clean it regularly
There is a big jump in how loud this thing gets when you go from speed 1 (37.6 dBA) to speed 2 (49.4 dBA)
It only has one fan speed below 45 decibels (speed 1) and its performance at this speed is weak (170 m3/h)

name: Mila Air 3
positives: quiet operation
small footprint
negatives: lower clean air delivery rate than Levoit Core 600S

name: Coway Airmega 200M
positives: adequate performance for small to medium rooms
particle ionizer to clump small particles (optional use)
three‐stage filtration system
negatives: no Wi-Fi connectivity
ionizer produces ozone byproduct

name: Coway Airmega 450
positives: Has a control-panel lock
Wheels and handle make it easy to move around
Quiet
negatives: No app control or remote
Warranty is short for the price
Air quality indicator lights are confusing

name: Rabbit Air BioGS 2.0
recommendations: Best Air Purifier for Wildfire Smoke
positives: Five-year warranty
Sleek looks
Has a remote
Charcoal filter removes odors and VOCs
negatives: Doesn't work with the Rabbit Air app
Doesn't work with smart-home platforms

name: Blueair ComfortPure 3-in-1
recommendations: Best Air Purifier for Those Who Also Need a Heater
positives: Indicator lights match the US air quality index
Has a fan and a heater
Easy-to-use app
negatives: Louder than expected
Panel doesn't consistently illuminate when pressed

name: Coway Airmega ProX
positives: It features four smart modes, giving you more flexibility based on how much power, noise, or energy consumption you want
It’s the fastest residential air purifier we tested, ranking 2nd after the commercial beast that is the Smart Air Blast – we estimate a PM1 CADR of 462 cfm
It is made of high-quality materials and looks great
It comes with an onboard air quality sensor and shows real-time particle readings on screen paired with a color-coded LED light at the top of the device
negatives: It doesn’t come with app connectivity, which some people might expect when paying upwards of $800
It is not cheap, with a list price just over $800
Occasionally, you may accidentally open the side covers when using the side handles to lift the ProX

name: Rabbit Air A3 Ultra Quiet
positives: Easy to use
Fun customizable panels
Quiet
negatives: Control panel turns off when not in use, creating an extra step
Mood lighting is confusing
Pricier than similar purifiers

name: AirDoctor Smart 5500i
positives: Quiet for a tower model
Connects to the AirDoctor app
Casters make it easy to move around
negatives: Shipping box is unwieldy
One-year warranty is scant for the price

name: Dyson Big+Quiet Formaldehyde BP06
positives: performs well
negatives: lacks convenience and automation features compared to top pick
no incremental fan speed adjustment; louder user experience

name: Austin Air HealthMate
positives: It has a one-dial operation that is easy to use right out of the box
Its body is made out of metal, so it’s durable but heavy and comes on high quality castor wheels
It comes with 15 lbs of activated carbon with zeolite, which is the largest amount we have seen in a household air purifier
Filters can last up to five years before they need to be replaced
negatives: At 61.5 dBA, it gets pretty loud when running at top speed
It has a high initial cost of just over $750 but comes with no smart features, no sensor or app support
The amount of carbon in the filter heavily restricts airflow, so it has the same CADR as the much smaller Levoit Core 300
At maximum fan speed, it pulls 148 watts, which is high compared to similar-performing air purifiers

name: IQAir Atem Earth
positives: Handle for easier moving
Environmentally friendly
Long warranty
Indicator light rings on the control panel that show both indoor and outdoor air
negatives: Indicator lights are hard to see on the flat top surface
Steep price

name: IQAir Atem X
positives: Has app control
10-year warranty
Quiet
negatives: No carbon filter to remove gases

name: IQAir GC MultiGas XE
positives: Intuitive and easy to use
10-year warranty
Effective for large square footage
Also captures toxic vapors and gases
negatives: Loud on high settings
Needs to be 1 foot away from a wall

name: CleanAirKits Triple Exhalaron
positives: It is compatible with generic PUREBURG filters, reducing yearly running costs
It looks cool with its industrial wooden design and colorful knob
It is super quiet, measuring 35.2 dB to 40.5 dB from its lowest to highest fan speed

name: Smart Air Blast
positives: Very fair prices for new filters that provide 13 months of usage
Only has a one-dial command to navigate through three fan speeds
It’s a set-and-forget unit without any apps or fancy modes, and you can use it with a smart plug
With an outer case made of metal instead of plastic, this unit is robust and built to last
It can clean a lot of air but only makes 54.7 dB(A) of sound at its top fan speed
negatives: It is an oversized unit that takes up a lot of space, making it likely overkill for most homes
It comes at a high initial price of just over $1,000
It’s also a heavy unit (38 kg), not designed to be moved around easily
It consumes significantly more energy than other large air purifiers, pulling 135.9 watts at top speed

name: AirFanta 3Pro
positives: It is the only high-CADR air purifier you can fit in a backpack
It comes with an AC-to-DC power adapter that includes a dial to adjust the voltage and sound levels
It offers the best performance for $ spent across the 130+ air purifiers we tested
You can choose between HEPA-only or HEPA with activated carbon
The use of PC fans makes it quieter and more energy-efficient than most traditional retail air purifiers
negatives: You will have to build it yourself, but it takes just a couple of minutes to do so
It has no smart features, no air quality sensor, and no app support
It’s in high demand, so it is often out of stock on Amazon
The design is not for everyone, with a subscriber describing it to me as “a sci-fi toaster”

name: Levoit Core 600S
positives: Wi-Fi enabled with air quality estimates and filter replacement alerts
substantial clean air delivery rate
negatives: louder than top pick at low fan speeds
tall unit footprint

name: Alen BreatheSmart 75i
positives: Excellent app dashboard
Surprisingly quiet
negatives: Awkward wheels for moving it around

name: Brondell O2+ Horizon
positives: Slim, understated design
Filters punch above their weight
Great value
negatives: No internal air sensor
No auto mode
Very loud at the highest setting
No app

name: NuWave OxyPure
positives: multi-stage filtration with washable and reusable filters
high clean air delivery rate
built-in particle sensors for automatic adjustments
Wi-Fi connectivity for remote operation
long-lasting components with 20-year lifespan except filters
quiet operation at low fan speeds
negatives: louder at higher fan speeds

name: Sharp FPF30UH
positives: Solid air cleaner at an inexpensive price
negatives: No internal air sensor
No auto mode
No app

name: CleanAirKits Luggable XL Ultra
positives: It offers excellent air cleaning power even under the 45 dBA limit we set for quiet performance tests (284 cfm)
If anything breaks, you will be able to fix it yourself
The body is made of lightweight wood, making it more sustainable than traditional plastic air cleaners
You can use any brand of 20×25 MERV 13 filters, which leads to lower running costs
It’s lightweight and comes with a handle for easy movement from room to room
You can buy the DIY kit, which is cheaper and includes everything you need to build it at home (except the filters)
negatives: It doesn’t come with carbon, so it won’t help with odors and chemical gases
It’s a large device that won’t fit in smaller spaces
The lightweight wood looks great, but the body is more delicate than plastic or metal you’ll find in other air purifiers
It doesn’t come with any smart functions or app support

name: Airpura R600 All-Purpose Air Purifier
positives: Designed and manufactured in Laval, Quebec
18 lb activated carbon filter for exceptional gas and odor removal
All-metal powder-coat steel housing, no plastic
Covers up to 2,000 sq ft at 560 CFM
No ozone, no ionizer, no unnecessary gimmicks
5-year parts / 10-year labor warranty
negatives: No smart features — no app, no AQI sensor, no auto mode
High filter replacement cost (~$240–265/year)
No AHAM CADR certification
45 lbs — heavy to move between rooms
notes: Canadian brand pick (not reviewer-backed). Airpura Industries, Laval, QC.
